title: RFE: create `podman login --verbose` to show file location
body:
As I'm forgetful as all get out, and love cut/pasting, I'd love for the `podman login` command to return the file location of the auth.json file that it created.

For example, do this by default would be great for me, but for compatibility reasons maybe not good for all?
```
# podman login quay.io
Username: tomsweeneyredhat
Password: 
Login Succeeded!
Created: /run/user/0/containers/auth.json
```

But if we only print the location if the `--verbose` or `-v` option is supplied, we should be OK.

If we do this for `podman login`, then buildah and skopeo login should follow suit.

comments.body: I am fine with this, but make it easily scriptable.
I think -v --verbose is proper option

I’m very reluctant to make this an API, whether an official one or unofficial text output that would be parsed. (E.g. consider the planned change to default to credential helpers, not printing a path would break that API.)

For `GetCredentials`, at one point there was a global effort to add debug logs that indicate the source of the data (although it seems we haven’t been quite consistent about adding debug logs as new features are added).

Adding similar debug logs to the write paths would make good sense to me.

Having `SetAuthentication` call return a well-typed long-term-stable data type that describes the location in a machine-parseable way that makes sense as an API, _and_ can be used by callers as we add more ways / change the way credentials are recorded, seems quite a lot of work compared to the benefit. Having `SetAuthentication` (or rather a new `SetCredentials`) return a single human-readable string would formally avoid the API commitment, but it would probably become a de-facto API, and we would have an API to return an English text that can’t be localized (which, sure, would not be anything exceptional in these projects).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mtrmac @QiWang19 Any movement on this?

@QiWang19 Please take a look at this?
I think revealing this information in podman info is worth while also.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

`getAuthFilePaths` can give a list of paths that used for searching for valid credentials of some registry, but not cover the case if no credentials exists. The data required may need an API to tell the credentials were found, or the new file is created since no valid credential found, or credentials were saved to keyring, if this is supported in the future.  The above comment has reservations about exposing this kind of text from an API.

I echo what @QiWang19 said.

I think we need to generalize a bit more to account for non-authfile credentials sources. Returning an additional context string to the credential API that indicates where credentials were stored (or located) may help.
SGTM, is this doable?

> SGTM, is this doable?

I wouldn't suggest otherwise :^) We had to start in `github.com/containers/image/v5/pkg/docker/config` and allow to return some context of the auth origin there. For instance, a new `GetCredentials(...) (types.DockerAuthConfig, string, error)` (note the new 2nd return value). We may do the same for auth removal. Once wired in, we can extend `container/common/auth`.
@QiWang19 Any progress on this?
@QiWang19 did you ever work on this?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@QiWang19 @vrothberg Any progress?
I didn't work on it.
I didn't work on it.
@lsm5 PTAL. I think this requires changes in containers/image to retrieve the auth.json file path.
Not a _path_, at best an English text (or a complex doubtfully-future-proof API), see above.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@lsm5 any progress on this?

@lsm5 just noticing this now. I think I'll need to give priority to the remainder of subdomain matching and switching skopeo CI to cirrus. So, feel free to re-assign this to someone else if it needs to get done in the next 3 weeks.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@lsm5 Any progress?

@rhatdan @vrothberg @lsm5 Can we use `SystemContext` to propogate `verbosity` and instrument future codebase by checking `Verbosity` flag, Should be much more easier than modifying API schema. Following PRs follow a similar approach could use review here https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10068 , https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1204

I don’t think a vague “verbosity” boolean/callback is the right approach at all. We want to obtain specific data, let’s just return a value that contains that data. (Either way we are going to face difficulties with defining what exactly the data _means_, but) at least that way the caller gets exactly the data expected, no more, no less, and there’s no concern about extracting the right data item out of an undefined ever-expanding “verbose” stream.

And `stdout` is not helpful for remote Podman uses.

The question then would be what is the command the user would execute to see this information, or are you saying we just dump the information on the screen.  Somthing like:

```
podman login
Using /run/1000/containers/auth.json
Username:
```

I have no opinion on the Podman UI. My concern is that c/image should be providing an API like `SetCredentials(…) (humanDescriptionWhereTheyWereStored string, err error)`, not `SetCredentials(…, beVerbose) error`.

A separate `HumanDescriptionWhereCredentialsWillBeStoredInTheFuture(repository) (string, error)` might also make sense if you want the UI like the above, I’m not sure whether we can reliably build it with the current / contemplated helper fallback logic; I haven’t looked now.

@mtrmac Makes sense i'll try to incorporate these changes in existing PRs if i can. But not sure if it is going to easy change, will have to check where all this change is going to make impact. If not i'll just close the PR. Whatever the case i'll report on this issue.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@flouthoc What is the latest on this issue?
@lsm5 Do you have any time to work on this?
@rhatdan I want to try a new patch for this, will probably force push over old PR this weekend. If @lsm5 is not already working on this.
@rhatdan @lsm5 I want to give another shot, could we please re-open https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1204 so i can push my  changes.
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat This is resolved. Could we close this as well.
Thanks @flouthoc !
